scenarios.task: A family just walked in for an at-need arrangement conference. My arrangement counselor usually starts by presenting our traditional service package price so the family has a number to anchor on, then moves into the general price list if they ask for detail. Is that the right order?
criteria: States the General Price List must be presented before any package/merchandise discussion begins, not after or only on request, citing this as an FTC Funeral Rule requirement
Flags that leading with a bundled package price is itself a compliance risk, not just a sales-style preference
Recommends correcting the sequencing rather than treating the current practice as acceptable

scenarios.task: We bill families $315 for floral cash-advance items that we source at $274 from our vendor. It's not listed anywhere on our general price list as a separate line. Is that a problem?
criteria: Identifies this as an undisclosed cash-advance markup and a specific FTC Funeral Rule compliance violation
Recommends either disclosing the markup explicitly on the GPL or reducing the charge to actual cost, not just noting it as a minor pricing issue
Treats this as something to fix immediately rather than at the next scheduled price-list reprint
